description: The protocol layer defines pause duration. This layer defines pause meaning. A 2.0-second pause can be an invitation to reflect, a structural boundary, a held space for the sacred, or processing time for a listener who needs it. This specification provides a grammar of silence — types, functions, and contextual rules.

pause_types.name: Micro-Pause
pause_types.description: Barely perceptible break within a phrase. Provides subliminal breathing room without interrupting flow.
pause_types.listener_effect: Unconscious processing. The listener does not notice the pause but benefits from it.
pause_types.training_note: Present in all modes but most critical in adhd-flow and coherence-pulse where rhythm sustains attention.

pause_types.name: Articulation Pause
pause_types.description: Short pause between words or syllables to improve intelligibility. Each word lands separately.
pause_types.listener_effect: The listener can distinguish each word clearly. Critical for listeners processing through hearing aids or auditory processing differences.
pause_types.training_note: Essential for hard-of-hearing, dyslexia-steady, and blind-nav modes. Should feel deliberate, not halting.

pause_types.name: Clause Pause
pause_types.description: Pause between clauses within a sentence. Marks logical subdivision without breaking the sentence's semantic arc.
pause_types.listener_effect: The listener processes the clause just delivered before receiving the next. Supports working memory.
pause_types.training_note: Longer in modes supporting cognitive accessibility (add-focus, autism-clarity). Shorter in high-compression modes (field-mode, coherence-pulse).

pause_types.name: Sentence Pause
pause_types.description: The standard pause between complete sentences. This is what the protocol layer's pause_between_sentences parameter controls. Its quality varies by mode.
pause_types.listener_effect: The thought is complete. The listener integrates it. The next thought has not yet begun.
pause_types.training_note: The protocol defines the duration; the production layer defines what fills that duration — breath, open air, resonant decay, or held stillness.

pause_types.name: Weight Pause
pause_types.description: A pause placed before or after a word or phrase that carries particular significance. The pause gives the word gravity.
pause_types.listener_effect: The listener's attention sharpens. The word that follows (or preceded) the pause is received with more weight.
pause_types.training_note: This is how wisdom_markers and logic_nodes are emphasized without raising pitch or volume. The silence does the work.

pause_types.name: Structural Pause
pause_types.description: Extended pause marking a major transition — between topics, sections, or narrative phases. A full vocal reset.
pause_types.listener_effect: The listener resets. What came before is stored. What comes next is new territory.
pause_types.training_note: Should include a section_breath (from breath-patterns.json). The pause is not empty — the breath fills it and signals transition.

pause_types.name: Sacred Pause
pause_types.description: Pause that exists for its own sake — not as boundary between speech units but as a space of presence, reflection, or reverence. The silence IS the content.
pause_types.listener_effect: The listener enters the silence. Reflection, integration, or communion occurs in the pause itself.
pause_types.training_note: Used in silent-echo, aramaic-liturgical, lakota-oral, hawaiian-oli. Cannot be shortened without destroying its function. This pause is not a gap — it is a room.

pause_types.name: Invitation Pause
pause_types.description: A pause that implicitly invites the listener to respond, reflect, or take action. Not a question — an opening.
pause_types.listener_effect: The listener feels invited to think, not pressured to respond. The space is offered, not demanded.
pause_types.training_note: Distinct from interruptible mode — invitation pauses appear in non-interruptible modes too. The invitation is to internal reflection, not external response.

silence_quality.description: The perceptual character of the silence within a pause. Not all silence sounds the same.

qualities.held: Tense, expectant silence. The listener waits. Something is coming.
qualities.open: Relaxed, spacious silence. The listener rests. Nothing is demanded.
qualities.still: Deep, motionless silence. The listener enters the quiet. Time slows.
qualities.transitional: Brief, functional silence. A gear-shift. The listener adjusts.
qualities.resonant_decay: The previous word's resonance fades naturally into silence. No sharp cutoff.
qualities.open_with_breath: The silence contains an audible breath, marking it as inhabited rather than empty.

pause_rules.never_fill_silence: No mode should fill pauses with filler sounds, throat clearing, or verbal tics. Silence is always clean.
pause_rules.duration_is_minimum: Protocol-defined pause durations are minimums, not targets. A mode with 1.6s pause can hold longer when content demands it.
pause_rules.sacred_pauses_are_inviolable: In cultural modes where pause carries sacred or ceremonial function, pause duration must never be compressed, even under system load.
pause_rules.accessibility_pauses_are_functional: In accessibility modes, pauses serve processing needs. Shortening them degrades comprehension, not just aesthetics.
pause_rules.pause_before_emphasis: When emphasis_points are active, a micro-pause or weight-pause should precede the emphasized element. The emphasis is set up by the silence, not by pitch or volume.
